title: PTZ Camera Preset Home and Auto-Flip Failure Monitoring
exclusions: Does not certify video chain-of-custody for legal evidence beyond log correlation, does not replace VMS-native health dashboards for storage or network throughput, and does not cover fixed-lens cameras lacking PTZ telemetry.
evidence: Splunk saved search or alert history for PTZ homing failures plus linked dashboard panels on `index=physical` showing per-`camera_id` `error_code` rollups exported as PDF or scheduled CSV to the physical security evidence share.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Force a lab Axis Q6075-LE paired to Milestone XProtect to abort a tour without returning home, generating repeated PTZ motor timeout lines with nonzero `error_code` for the same `camera_id` across two Splunk hourly buckets while `preset=home` never succeeds.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Operators run an approved Milestone maintenance tour that intentionally pauses on a preset for training; Splunk sees single-hour spikes but CMMS/KVStore suppressions mark the window, so the alert stays quiet despite transient `motor` chatter without sustained `error_code` accumulation. Matching CMMS ticket IDs prove the outage was planned.

dataSources: Land Axis, Hanwha, Hikvision SDK diagnostics or Milestone Recording Server bridges into `index=physical` using **`sourcetype=cctv:ptz`** (and companion **`sourcetype=nvr:diagnostic`** lines where the VMS emits PTZ health separately) with Splunk-extracted fields **`preset`**, **`home`**, **`timeout`**, **`motor`**, and **`error_code`** tied to **`camera_id`**. Typical ingest uses syslog/TLS forwarded through **Splunk Connect for Syslog** ([Splunkbase app **4740**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4740)), Heavy Forwarder `inputs.conf` listeners, or **`POST /services/collector/event`** HEC JSON when SDK exporters emit structured payloads.
app: Axis, Hanwha, Hikvision PTZ SDK logs via syslog, or Milestone device logs
spl:
index=physical sourcetype=cctv:ptz OR (sourcetype=nvr:diagnostic PTZ)
| search preset OR Home OR timeout OR motor OR stall OR "PTZ error"
| stats count by host, camera_id, error_code
| where count > 5
| sort - count
description: Detects PTZ units that repeatedly fail homing after patrol tours complete—motor stalls, preset timeouts, or controller contention logged while frames stay pointed away from guard routes so perimeter analytics lose continuity.
value: Protects perimeter assurance for data halls and fenced compounds by shortening mean-time-to-repair on mechanical PTZ faults before patrol narratives or badge-correlation reviews contradict what cameras actually framed.
implementation: Tier diagnostics verbosity on noisy Axis/Hanwha/Hikvision bodies first; normalize vendor integers into **`error_code`** via transforms lookup tables Milestone administrators align with Device Pack release notes. Schedule the hourly aggregation saved search under the physical-security service account, alert when Splunk counts failures/hour stay above five for two rolling hours per camera, and optionally enrich outdoor domes with approved weather CSV lookups gated behind RBAC.
visualization: Primary `splunk.table` of latest hour: `camera_id`, `site`, `error_code`, failure count, linked drilldown to raw `cctv:ptz` events; companion `splunk.line` trending failures per camera; optional `splunk.singlevalue` KPI for cameras breaching threshold; geo context only when CMDB coordinates exist.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Forwarding and receiving syslog
ONVIF — Open Network Video Interface Forum

knownFalsePositives: **Milestone XProtect scheduled patrol scripts** deliberately sweep presets—suppress via KVStore **`ptz_maintenance`** rows keyed by `camera_id` plus `_time` windows sourced from SOC change calendars. **Axis Device Manager bulk firmware pushes** flood transient motor warnings—require two consecutive trending hours before paging or whitelist `job_id` markers emitted during upgrades. **Hanwha/Wisenet dome self-diagnostics after weather or surge events** mimic stalls until the head clears—differentiate using short-duration bursts that lack repeated `error_code` in a second hour or tie to published maintenance windows. **Hikvision privacy-mask or scene-cutover commands** issued from the VMS resemble failed homing—ignore spikes that align with operator mask changes logged in the same `camera_id` stream while no tour-completion event is present.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Approved syslog/TLS or HEC path from Milestone Recording Server, Axis Companion/Device Manager feeds, Hanwha diagnostic bridges, or Hikvision SDK exporters into Splunk Cloud or Splunk Enterprise—Splunk Connect for Syslog ([Splunkbase app 4740](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4740)) on a hardened Heavy Forwarder is the usual pattern alongside documented **`inputs.conf`** UDP/TCP stanzas.
• Require normalized events on **`index=physical`** with **`sourcetype=cctv:ptz`** plus optional **`sourcetype=nvr:diagnostic`** rows referencing the same **`camera_id`**; extracted tokens must include **`preset`**, **`home`**, **`timeout`**, **`motor`**, **`error_code`**, and **`host`** before promoting alerting.
• RBAC: constrain dashboards and scheduled searches to a **`physical_security_analyst`** (or similarly scoped) role with search/filter limits on **`index=physical`** only; store integration secrets outside SPL literals.
• CMMS linkage fields (`work_order_id`, `vendor_case`) simplify tuning once mechanical remediation loops mature.

Step 1 — Configure collection
Forward verbose PTZ traces during calibration windows only; otherwise rely on Milestone audit bridges or Axis/Hanwha/Hikvision SDK heartbeat logs at vendor-documented intervals. For JSON-capable exporters, bind **`POST /services/collector/event`** HEC tokens with explicit sourcetype overrides so multi-line syslog fragmentation cannot split **`PTZ error`** strings.

Step 2 — Build validation searches
Before enabling automation, run ad hoc searches over **`index=physical`** **`sourcetype=cctv:ptz`** with **`camera_id`** split-by counts inside a **`-24h`** window to capture baseline jitter when icing or lubrication cycles occur; repeat against **`sourcetype=nvr:diagnostic`** if Milestone duplicates alarms.

Step 3 — Validate against consoles
Cross-check spikes against Milestone Smart Client playback timelines and Axis/Hanwha/Hikvision maintenance utilities so Splunk cardinality mirrors observed mechanical faults rather than benign UI retries.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Publish dashboard drilldowns (camera × **`error_code`**, hourly failure sparklines, geo overlays fed by CMDB latitude/longitude lookups) and route breaches into CMMS tickets referencing stored Splunk SID URLs as forensic breadcrumbs.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
